_meta.doc: Per-surface kanji exceptions: kanji NOT in n5_kanji_whitelist.json that ARE allowed to appear in specific surfaces of the corpus. Surfaces: 'dokkai-passage' (reading passages where authentic JLPT N5 texts use these), 'paper-distractor' (mock-paper question distractors where non-N5 kanji deliberately exercise recognition under exam-like conditions). Each entry tags its applicable surfaces; JA-28 enforces dokkai, JA-40 enforces paper distractors.
_meta.previous_schema: v1 was {exception_kanji: [<kanji>]} flat array. v2 is [{kanji, addedAt, reason, surfaces, paper_occurrences?}]. Migrated 2026-05-08 per ISSUE-007 + IMP-005.
_meta.rule_for_dokkai: data/papers/dokkai/*.json passages MAY use kanji here with surfaces including "dokkai-passage". data/reading.json (older Reading-practice tab) STAYS strict N5.
_meta.rule_for_paper_distractor: data/papers/{moji,goi,bunpou}/*.json questions MAY use kanji here with surfaces including "paper-distractor", but only as recognition-only distractors — never as the correct answer. JA-40 enforces both.
_meta.how_to_add: When adding a new exception, include: kanji char, addedAt date, one-line reason, surfaces array. If the same kanji is allowed in multiple surfaces, list them all in the same entry. Re-run tools/check_content_integrity.py to confirm JA-28 + JA-40 still pass.
see_also: tools/check_content_integrity.py (JA-28 dokkai enforcement, JA-40 paper-distractor enforcement)
data/papers/dokkai/ (current authoring location — KnowledgeBank source deleted 2026-05-14)
